callRationale: Started with a docs-only search objective for Firecrawl Search API parameters on docs.firecrawl.dev.
Fetched the official Search feature page and Search API reference after the search returned the canonical docs URL.
Made a narrower API reference fetch for timeout and ignoreInvalidURLs because the first fetch did not surface ignoreInvalidURLs clearly.
Made a final targeted fetch for categories so the final answer could cite official docs-only evidence for all requested fields.
strengths: The provider found the canonical official Search API reference page and official Search feature page.
Targeted fetches returned concise, directly relevant excerpts for categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs.
The final answer uses only docs.firecrawl.dev evidence.
weaknesses: The provider has no explicit source-policy control in the exposed schema.
The initial docs-only web_search still returned one off-domain result from lobehub.com.
Fetching the OpenAPI JSON URL produced a fetch_error despite HTTP 200, so the usable evidence came from the rendered API reference page.
notableFailures: Source policy was not fully adherent at the retrieval level because the first search result set included an off-domain page.
The first broad full_content fetch was very large and still did not clearly extract ignoreInvalidURLs, requiring a narrower follow-up fetch.
